diagrams.title: Object Lifecycle Timeline
diagrams.description: A horizontal timeline showing the three phases of a C++ object: Creation (Constructor), Usage, and Destruction (Destructor). Arrows indicate the flow of time, with callout boxes showing when constructors and destructors are invoked relative to the object's scope.
elements: Horizontal timeline arrow from left to right
Three labeled sections: Object Creation, Object Usage, Object Destruction
Constructor callout box at start point with function icon
Destructor callout box at end point with function icon
Object representation in the middle section as a C++ class instance
Scope boundary indicators showing object lifetime
diagrams.purpose: Helps students understand when constructors and destructors are called in the lifetime of an object, establishing the foundation for memory management.

diagrams.title: Types of Constructors
diagrams.description: Four parallel code block representations showing Default, Parameterized, Copy, and Move constructors. Each block shows the constructor signature and a brief example usage. Icons differentiate each type (gear for default, parameters icon, copy icon, arrow icon).
elements: Four vertical panels side by side
Code syntax blocks for each constructor type
ClassName() - Default constructor panel
ClassName(parameters) - Parameterized constructor panel
ClassName(const ClassName&) - Copy constructor panel
ClassName(ClassName&&) - Move constructor panel
Color coding: blue for default, green for parameterized, orange for copy, purple for move
Small usage examples below each signature
diagrams.purpose: Clearly distinguishes the four constructor types students must learn, reinforcing syntax differences and use cases through visual categorization.

diagrams.title: Constructor & Destructor Call Order in Inheritance
diagrams.description: A class hierarchy diagram with a base class and derived class. Numbered callout bubbles show the order of execution: 1-Base Constructor, 2-Derived Constructor, 3-Derived Destructor, 4-Base Destructor. This demonstrates the reverse order of destruction and construction in inheritance.
elements: Two stacked rectangles representing Base Class and Derived Class
Inheritance arrow from Derived to Base
Numbered circular badges (1, 2, 3, 4) positioned at constructor/destructor call points
Vertical flow arrows showing execution sequence
Color gradient from top (earlier) to bottom (later)
Destructor callout text: 'Reverse order - Derived destructor runs BEFORE Base destructor'
Constructor callout text: 'Base constructor runs FIRST, then Derived constructor'
diagrams.purpose: Visualizes the critical concept of constructor/destructor order in inheritance - that destructors run in reverse order of constructors, preventing resource leaks and undefined behavior.
